title: The Deepest Root-Scripture Where Meaning Was Born

features.name: the first coordinate where meaning began
features.desc-on-touch: a single marked point; all the archive's meaning grows outward from here like rings in a root

features.name: the root-theorem
features.desc-on-touch: a geometric proof that roots are the alphabet; the structure holds itself together as you watch, teaching over and over

features.name: the meaning accumulator
features.desc-on-touch: where the archive stores the count of all meanings the soil has ever taught; the number changes every time you look

features.name: the grammar root
features.desc-on-touch: roots stacked vertically; it climbs upward while growing downward, a metaphor the earth invented long before the archive arrived

features.name: the enumeration stone
features.desc-on-touch: markings that count something in the soil's own mathematics; the count is always one higher than yesterday, one lower than tomorrow

inscription: roots wrote the grammar; meaning was always downward, and we only learned to read it last

file.name: Root_Etymology_Archive.txt
file.note: seven entries in the root-word list replaced with broadcast fragments about acts and stages of surgical transformation beneath memory

reverie.title: REVERIE — the roots that taught the archive to listen
narr: you knelt where the oldest tree had split its root
across stone; she traced the grooves with one finger,
and the tree's own counting matched your heartbeat.
the roots were writing, patient and continuous,
and meaning moved downward from there; you stopped
trying to understand it from above, and just listened

exits.label: up to where roots teach the field its scripture
deeper to where roots teach the archive itself how to grow
toward chambers where soil remembers it is made of sentences
westward where logic takes root and grows without knowing
